name: Freeman Bus Corp
owner_or_parent: Hale Transportation Group (Hale's Bus Garage LLC)
principals: Acquired from Robert C. Freeman III (2019 sale to Leif Petterson/Norfe Systems); then Hale Transportation acquired Jan 2023
districts_or_customers: Historically Watertown City School District (lost decades-old contract ~2019); regional Jefferson County NY school routes plus Clarence Henry Coach charter line
recent_news: Jan 2023: Acquired by Hale Transportation Group / Hale's Bus Garage LLC (Cooperstown NY area); transaction included Clarence Henry Coach, Freeman School Bus Corp., and Freeman Fleet Repairs; 20 employees retained. Prior 2019 sale by Robert Freeman III to consultant Leif Petterson.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — already rolled up under Hale Transportation Group (strategic NY regional operator) as of Jan 2023. Address in source data (Philadelphia, NY Route 11) likely a secondary garage; main operation Watertown NY. Exclude from active prospect list.

name: Mckinley & City Line Bus Co
districts_or_customers: Likely School District of Philadelphia (Philadelphia uses ~18 private contractors at 22 garages); North Philadelphia location (4341 N 7th St, 19140) suggests inner-city Philadelphia public/parochial school routes
recent_news: No public M&A, ownership, or news coverage located. Company has no discoverable website, no Facebook/Yelp presence, no FMCSA snapshot under this exact name in available results; no obvious lead executive listed in source dataset.
fit_notes: Low-visibility small Philadelphia school bus contractor (~36 employees, ~$1.7M rev). Likely independent family-owned operator serving School District of Philadelphia; obscure online footprint suggests succession risk could be material but requires direct outreach to confirm ownership, fleet, and contracts. Worth a discreet call before prioritization.

name: Kauffman Bus Svc Inc
principals: Jason Petroff (Manager, per source); historical contact W. Hepler (whepler@epix.net); Kauffman family name suggests founding-family roots
districts_or_customers: Cumberland Valley School District; Northern York County School District (Cumberland/York county PA corridor near Mechanicsburg/Harrisburg)
recent_news: Active recruiting for school bus drivers and van drivers in 2024-2025 with paid CDL training and sign-on bonuses; FMCSA DOT 907104 shows 63 power units / 63 CDL drivers (school bus carrier classification); no M&A or sale news located.
fit_notes: Solid independent PA contractor — ~63 buses serving Cumberland Valley & Northern York County SDs, both stable affluent suburban Harrisburg districts. Name suggests Kauffman family legacy with day-to-day run by Jason Petroff; medium succession potential. Geographic fit for Summit PA platform; strong tuck-in candidate.

name: Central Bucks Trnsprtn Inc
principals: Operated by 'Steve and Tom' per local references; full surnames not publicly disclosed
districts_or_customers: Bucks County PA districts — likely contracted to area public/non-public schools (Central Bucks SD area, though district itself self-operates much of its yellow-bus service; CBT likely serves act-372 non-public routes or feeder routes). Trevose/Feasterville location suggests Bensalem/Bristol/Neshaminy footprint
recent_news: No M&A or significant news located. ZoomInfo lists company with 20-49 employees and $1M-$5M revenue range. Limited public footprint, no website found.
fit_notes: Small independent PA operator in attractive suburban Philadelphia (Bucks County) market. Two-principal owner-operator structure ('Steve and Tom') suggests classic mom-and-pop with possible succession opening. Direct call needed to confirm fleet, contracts, and ownership; geographically attractive for Summit PA expansion.

name: Menomonie Transportation Inc
principals: Dave Howe (President) and Ryan Howe (owners, 3rd generation); co-founder Maurice Howe (1946)
districts_or_customers: School District of the Menomonie Area (SDMA) - primary contract; Dunn County WI routes; some University of Wisconsin-Stout charter/community work
recent_news: 3rd-generation Howe family business, operating since 1946. 2018 highlighted in Sunshine Award recognition for employee tenure. A 2018 fatal crash involving a Menomonie Transportation vehicle (killed two Dunn County men) led to a trial; no business-impacting news beyond that. No M&A activity located.
website: menomonietransportation.com (source lists 'Sdmaonline.Com' which is the school district, not the company)
fit_notes: Classic 3rd-gen WI family operator (~50 employees, est. 1946) tied to single anchor district (SDMA) plus Dunn County charter. Howe family transition from Dave to Ryan in progress — moderate succession signal. Outside Summit's core state footprint (WI not flagged as Summit/expansion state in source), so geographic fit weaker but acquirable tuck-in if Summit pursues Upper Midwest entry.
